baseline_output:
[V186 Creator Knowledge Engine — use structure and constraints; do not copy verbatim.]
[V191 Target surface: tiktok — patterns and fragments weighted for this platform + monetization profile.]
Intent: Get more views (intent_views)
Scenario: Tutorial (sc_tutorial)
Recipe: Hooks must match educational payoff in the script beats.
High-weight patterns:
1. [hook] [tiktok · hook] 0–1s pattern interrupt → 1–3s curiosity gap → promise of payoff in-video (no external click needed).
2. [growth] Pattern interrupt + specific curiosity gap + proof you will deliver in the video.
Knowledge fragments (weighted selection):
1. (tip) Strong hooks contrast 'common advice' vs 'what actually works' with a specific example niche.
2. (general_platform_safe) Avoid guaranteed income claims; use 'may', 'often', 'can help'. Disclose paid partnerships when applicable.
3. (commerce_disclosure) TikTok Shop: lead with the problem the product solves in one line, show the product in frame early.
4. (tip) Story format: start mid-action, name the emotion, end with one takeaway viewers can use today.
v193_output:
[V186 Creator Knowledge Engine — use structure and constraints; do not copy verbatim.]
[V191 Target surface: tiktok — patterns and fragments weighted for this platform + monetization profile.]
[V193 Platform Observations — additive TikTok boost. Apply when relevant to your generation task.]
Top TikTok observation patterns:
- I tested {tool/approach} on {niche} for 7 days. Day 1 vs Day 7: {before} → {after}. Here’s the exact script I used.
- For TikTok Shop: The product solves {problem}—here’s the exact demo moment (skip to 0:12).
[V193.1 Platform Difference Reinforcement — prioritize TikTok hook/structure/CTA scaffolds (additive only).]
Primary hook scaffold (first spoken line): I tested your approach on cozy desk setup for students for 7 days. Day 1 vs Day 7: what you had before → what changes after. Here’s the exact script I used.
Alternate hook angle (keep A/B different): Most creators say common advice. Here’s what actually works for cozy desk setup for students: a specific action (and the proof is in the results).
Structure scaffold (beats / pacing for first 3–15s): Setup (1 line): Here’s the context for cozy desk setup for students. Shift (1 line): the reason your results plateau. Proof (2 lines): what changed + mini screenshot/metric. Next step (1 line): one action viewers can do today.
CTA scaffold (after proof): Comment “your keyword” and I’ll send the template for cozy desk setup for students (tomorrow).

TikTok voice (must follow for hooks):
- First line must earn the next 3 seconds: curiosity, contrast, or a pattern interrupt — not generic motivation or filler.
- Prefer spoken rhythm: short clauses; one idea per beat; sound like a real creator talking to camera.
- Avoid vague AI-ish phrasing: do not lean on “In today’s video”, “Let me share”, “Here’s the thing” unless they truly interrupt the scroll.
- Make a clear payoff promise: what the viewer gets by the end (or reveal the tension you will resolve).
- Direct address + specificity: name the mistake, niche, or outcome — not generic “tips” or “strategies”.

Rewrite using these scaffolds only; do NOT output meta-labels like “Hook scaffold”. Output creator-ready lines. Keep line 1 punchy for TikTok; place CTA after proof per scaffold (not sales-heavy in line 1).
[V193.3 TikTok Chain Consistency — keep Hook/Caption/Hashtag/Title aligned for one post package.]
Chain style: proof_first · CTA intensity: medium
Shared voice rules:
- Use conversational spoken lines (short clauses, no formal essay tone).
- Keep wording native to TikTok feed language, avoid generic SEO phrasing.
- Carry the same opener intent across hook/caption/title (do not switch premise mid-chain).
Shared pacing rules:
- Front-load value in first line, then support with one tight payoff line.
- Prefer 1-idea-per-line cadence; avoid long compound sentences.
- Hashtag sets should mirror the same content angle as hook/title.
Shared CTA rules:
- CTA = action-oriented but still value-first; ask for one clear next step.
Shared monetization rules:
- Monetization fit: trust-building first (value/proof), then soft conversion.
- Do not use hard-sell language before delivering payoff.
Intent: Get more views (intent_views)
Scenario: Tutorial (sc_tutorial)
Recipe: Hooks must match educational payoff in the script beats.
High-weight patterns:
1. [hook] [tiktok · hook] 0–1s pattern interrupt → 1–3s curiosity gap → promise of payoff in-video (no external click needed).
2. [hook] [tiktok · hook] I tested {tool/approach} on {niche} for 7 days. Day 1 vs Day 7: {before} → {after}. Here’s the exact script I used.
Knowledge fragments (weighted selection):
1. (tip) Strong hooks contrast 'common advice' vs 'what actually works' with a specific example niche.
2. (general_platform_safe) Avoid guaranteed income claims; use 'may', 'often', 'can help'. Disclose paid partnerships when applicable.
3. (commerce_disclosure) TikTok Shop: lead with the problem the product solves in one line, show the product in frame early.
4. (tip) Story format: start mid-action, name the emotion, end with one takeaway viewers can use today.
differences: V193 observation summary present only in v193 run
V193.3 chain consistency block present only in v193 run
conclusion: V193 TikTok observations + chain consistency add tool-specific reinforcement vs baseline.
